destinations.evidence: Delivery settlement creation verifies and records the raw executor-signed delivery result as the SPC output.

equivalence_argument: The callback payload may be ABI-packed for ZK, but the SPC verification and delivery_spc_call output use raw_delivery_result, preserving the signed bytes.
notes: The mapping distinguishes callback encoding from SPC output bytes.
rationale: The delivery SpcCall construction changes from delivery_result to raw_delivery_result, with a comment that it is what the executor signed before ZK packing. The commit body identifies ABI-packed output versus raw output as the root cause of verifier-side SPC signature mismatches. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Delivery SPC calls must use the raw delivery result bytes that the executor signed as the SPC output, not an ABI-packed settlement output.
suggested_falsification_tests: Submit a delivery result whose raw bytes differ from the ABI-packed settlement output, build the delivery SPC call, and assert the SPC output equals the raw executor-signed bytes and verifies with the executor proof.
summary: Delivery SPC calls use the raw executor-signed result.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Use a ZK delivery result where raw output differs from callback ABI packing and assert delivery_spc_call.output equals the raw result.
title: Delivery SPC signs raw result
